items.title: Quoting Anthropic

items.summary: The free open source agentic program is finally invading your phone.

items.title: Claude Science is Anthropic’s newest flagship product

items.title: What's new in Claude Sonnet 5
items.summary: What's new in Claude Sonnet 5 Claude Sonnet 5 came out this morning . Anthropic say of Sonnet 5 that "its performance is close to that of Opus 4.8, but at lower prices"....

items.title: The DeepMind trio who built a poker AI are now making money for quant hedge funds
items.summary: EquiLibre Technologies, a Prague-based AI lab founded by three ex-DeepMind researchers, is now valued at more than $500 million.

items.title: Anthropic's new Claude Sonnet 5 closes the gap to the pricier Opus model series
items.summary: Anthropic released Claude Sonnet 5, which beats its predecessor Sonnet 4.6 across all benchmarks and even edges past the larger Opus 4.8 on the GDPval-AA v2 knowledge work test with a score of 1,618. Anthropic is also quick to point out that the model scores far below the models the US government currently has blocked...

items.title: Introducing Claude Sonnet 5 on AWS: Anthropic’s most capable Sonnet model
items.summary: Today, we’re excited to announce the availability of Anthropic’s most advanced Sonnet model, Claude Sonnet 5, on Amazon Bedrock and Claude Platform on AWS. Claude Sonnet 5 is the first Sonnet model of Anthropic’s latest generation and represents a meaningful step forward. It delivers top-tier intelligence at Sonnet...

Anthropic launches Claude Science, an AI workspace built specifically for researchers
items.summary: Anthropic released Claude Science, an AI workbench for researchers. More than 60 preconfigured skills cover fields like genomics and computational chemistry, and a verification agent automatically checks citations and calculations. The app runs locally or on HPC clusters, so sensitive data never has to leave a lab's...

items.title: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents
items.summary: Anthropic’s Claude Sonnet 5 brings stronger agentic capabilities, lower pricing, and improved safety, positioning the model as a cheaper alternative to Opus, GPT-5.5, and Gemini Pro.

items.title: OpenAI reportedly cut response costs for guest ChatGPT users by more than half
items.summary: According to a report by The Information, OpenAI has cut inference costs for its AI models by more than half. The company applied the optimizations to ChatGPT, where the number of Nvidia GPUs needed dropped to just a few hundred at times. The article OpenAI reportedly cut response costs for guest ChatGPT users by more...

items.title: Google launches Nano Banana 2 Lite for fast AI images and Gemini Omni Flash for video via API
items.summary: Google adds two new generative AI models. Nano Banana 2 Lite generates images in four seconds at $0.034 a pop. Gemini Omni Flash brings video generation and editing via text prompts to the API for the first time. Google recommends chaining both models to go from a quick image to an animated video. items.title: Anthropic’s Claude Science bets on workflow, not a new model, to win over scientists
items.summary: Anthropic's Claude Science is a workbench that gives scientists one environment to do computational research, saving them from the need to bounce between databases, pipelines, and tools.

items.title: How Outpost VFX Uses AWS to Accelerate AI Model Training for Visual Effects
items.summary: In this post, we explore how Outpost VFX achieved 8x faster training speeds using AWS infrastructure to transform their face replacement workflow, the technical architecture they implemented to overcome single-GPU limitations, and the measurable results achieved through AWS multi-GPU training.

items.title: Fine-tune Amazon Nova models for accurate email data extraction

items.title: Q&A: What is agentic AI today, and what do we want it to be?
items.summary: Computer scientist Phillip Isola cuts through the hype to explain how AI agents work and what the future might hold for this rapidly advancing technology.

items.title: Meituan's LongCat-2.0 shows China can train massive AI models without Nvidia
items.summary: Meituan trains a 1.6 trillion parameter AI model entirely on Chinese chips, no Nvidia required. The article Meituan's LongCat-2.0 shows China can train massive AI models without Nvidia appeared first on The Decoder .

items.title: San Francisco's AI boom is pricing out six-figure tech workers who can't find rent under $5,000
items.summary: San Francisco's AI boom is driving up the cost of living so fast that even couples earning $365,000 a year can't find an affordable apartment. items.title: Amazon launches new $1 billion FDE org, following OpenAI and Anthropic
items.summary: Engineers on the new team will embed within companies to deploy purpose-built agents, focusing on fast deployments and customer self-sufficiency.

items.title: Building Local AI Systems: Qwen3.6 + MCPs

items.title: US campaigns now run on AI at nearly every step, and Europe is drawing a harder line
items.summary: Republican and Democratic campaigns now run on AI at nearly every step, from vetting opponents to micro-targeting voters, according to a New York Times report. items.title: Why the AI future won’t be decided by algorithms and chatbots
items.summary: When people talk about the race for artificial intelligence, they usually focus on software. Headlines revolve around ChatGPT, Gemini, DeepSeek or the latest breakthrough model. items.summary: Meta reportedly had hundreds of contractors pose as minors and send suicide, sex, and drug-related prompts to chatbots from OpenAI, Google, and Character.AI.
